name: Gemini 3 Flash
archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Transformer (natively multimodal, based on Gemini 3 Pro)
note: Pro-grade reasoning at Flash latency/cost via an adjustable thinking_level parameter; default model in the Gemini app.

results.notes: AIME 2025, no tools (99.7% with code execution)
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools (43.5% with search+code exec)
LiveCodeBench Pro, Elo rating (not a 0-100 pass rate)
CharXiv Reasoning, no tools, information synthesis from complex charts.
FACTS Benchmark Suite (grounding, parametric, search, and multimodal factuality).

verificationNotes:
Directly downloaded and read the official Gemini 3 Flash Model Card PDF ('Published: December 2025'), which includes a comparison table with a Gemini 3 Flash (Thinking) column. Every claimed score matches exactly: HLE 33.7% (45.8/43.5% is actually the search+code-exec figure — confirmed 43.5% specifically, not 45.8 which belongs to Gemini 3 Pro), GPQA 90.4, AIME 95.2% (99.7% with code execution), MMMU-Pro 81.2, SWE-Bench 78.0, MMMLU 91.8, LiveCodeBench Pro Elo 2,316. Release date Dec 17, 2025 corroborated by TechCrunch, 9to5Google, and blog.google. Pricing $0.50/$3.00 confirmed via multiple independent trackers (pricepertoken, OpenRouter) plus consistent with Google's per-tier structure. Model card confirms it is 'based on Gemini 3 Pro,' matching archDisplay wording.

2026-07-16: Checked all four missing core slugs (math, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu) against (1) official materials — the Gemini 3 Flash Model Card PDF, blog.google launch post, and ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/gemini-3 — none report MATH/MATH-500, HumanEval, base LiveCodeBench, or base MMMU; Google's own comparison table only carries the successor/harder variants already in this file (MMMU-Pro 81.2, LiveCodeBench Pro Elo 2316); (2) independent leaderboards — Vals.ai has no HumanEval tracker at all and lists MATH-500 as an archived/retired benchmark with no Gemini 3 Flash entry, and its "/benchmarks/mmmu" URL is actually titled "MMMU Pro" (already covered by our mmmu-pro entry) with no separate base-MMMU leaderboard; Artificial Analysis evaluates this model only via its composite Intelligence/Coding indices (GDPval, Tau-Banking, Terminal-Bench, SciCode, HLE, GPQA, CritPt, Omniscience, AA-LCR) and does not publish standalone MATH/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/MMMU scores for it; Epoch AI's public model page shows only the composite ECI, and its detailed benchmark-hub table is JS-rendered and did not yield a citable Gemini 3 Flash row for any of the four; (3) sibling cross-reference — the Gemini 3 Pro corpus file has none of these four slugs either (family-wide omission), and Gemini 3.5 Flash's corpus 'mmmu' value traces to a pricepertoken.com page whose number (83) sits suspiciously close to that sibling's self-reported MMMU-Pro score (83.6), i.e. it looks like a Pro-variant mislabel rather than a genuine base-MMMU measurement, so it was not used as precedent here. HumanEval and MATH-500 are widely treated as saturated/retired at the frontier (matches this corpus's own benchmark-catalog description), and both LiveCodeBench and MMMU have been superseded in Google's and most trackers' reporting by the Pro variants already recorded for this model. Conclusion: treating all four as a legitimate absence rather than a research failure; no scores added.

2026-07-17 (round-2, post-obvious-sources pass for 'math' and 'humaneval'): Checked all 6 new source types specified for this pass (HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, GitHub eval-harness logs, other leaderboards/aggregators, non-English sources, sibling-model comparison tables). No credible new score surfaced for either gap; both remain a legitimate absence.

(1) Stanford HELM: Fetched crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest/, /helm/mmlu/latest/, and /helm/capabilities/latest/, plus a benchgecko.ai HELM MMLU-Pro mirror. Search-engine snapshots and direct fetches show HELM's public leaderboards have NOT been updated with any Gemini 3-series model — Gemini 2.0 Flash and Claude 3.7 Sonnet (20250219) are still shown at the top of HELM Capabilities, and multiple sources confirm HELM entered maintenance mode on 2026-06-01. No 'gemini-3-flash' row exists on HELM Lite, HELM MMLU, or HELM Capabilities.

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar: Searched and fetched ~8 papers that name-check 'Gemini 3 Flash'/'Gemini-3-Flash' as a baseline: the Soohak research-math benchmark paper (arxiv 2605.09063, reports only SOOHAK-Mini/Challenge/Refusal scores for Gemini-3-Flash, no MATH/MMLU/HumanEval/AIME), the RSA/ARC-AGI-2 paper (arxiv 2509.26626v2, ARC-AGI-2 only), a 33-model metacognition atlas (arxiv 2605.06673, reports only a domain AUROC of 0.681, no standard benchmark table), UrduMMLU (arxiv 2606.07167, only has Gemini 3.5 Flash, not 3), LibEvoBench (arxiv 2606.25402) and an iterative self-repair paper (arxiv 2604.10508) whose tables reference Gemini model families generically but don't extractably name a 'Gemini 3 Flash' HumanEval row. None report the standard MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/AIME numbers for this exact model.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs: Found a real, citable candidate — github.com/timini/ensemble, path packages/eval/.cache/baselines/google_gemini-3-flash-preview_math500.json (createdAt 2026-02-19), a committed baseline log with raw model completions and per-question numeric grading of 'google:gemini-3-flash-preview' (same release-date/model as this corpus entry per OpenRouter's page) against a 100-question MATH-500 subsample. Computed accuracy = 69/100 (69.0%). However, cross-checked the SAME repo's baseline logs for benchmarks we can verify against Google's own self-reported numbers: gpqa.json gives 80/100=80.0% (vs. official self-reported 90.4%) and hle.json gives 25/100=25.0% (vs. official self-reported 33.7%) — both diverge by 10+ points from the vendor's own figures already in this corpus. Manual inspection of individual MATH-500 entries also found a demonstrable grader bug (question math500-394: model answered '-3/8', evaluator's numeric parser recorded expected='8', predicted='-3/8', and marked it incorrect, even though '-3/8' exactly matches the stated ground truth '-\frac{3}{8}'). Given the small subsample (100 of 500), no published methodology, a confirmed grading bug, and a large unexplained gap vs. cross-checkable official numbers from the same harness, this source was judged NOT credible enough to add to the corpus and was rejected rather than used.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter's dedicated benchmarks page (openrouter.ai/google/gemini-3-flash-preview/benchmarks) was fetched directly and contains no benchmark scores at all (empty). LiveBench.ai's own historical CSV export and web search turned up no Gemini 3-series entry (still only 2.0-era Gemini rows). livecodebench.github.io/leaderboard.html is client-side JS-rendered and returned no accessible model list via fetch. PapersWithCode — no dedicated leaderboard entry found for this model via targeted search. Scale AI SEAL/Showdown — Gemini 3 Flash appears only on the Elo-style 'Showdown' popularity leaderboard (rank 3, score 1038.67 from 3,642 battles), not on any SEAL per-category (math/coding) leaderboard, and neither carries MMLU/MATH/HumanEval numbers. Several SEO/content-farm 'AI model directory' pages (automatio.ai and similar) do display MMLU (88.2% on one page, 78.9% on another) and HumanEval (92%) numbers for 'Gemini 3 Flash,' but these are mutually inconsistent across pages, unsourced/without visible methodology, and don't match Google's own self-reported figures for benchmarks we can cross-check (e.g. their 'MMMLU' framing vs. our recorded 91.8) — judged non-credible content-farm noise and not used, consistent with this corpus's existing caution about similar low-quality aggregator numbers (see pricepertoken.com note elsewhere in this file).

(5) Non-English sources: Ran targeted Chinese (谷歌 跑分) and Japanese (ベンチマーク) search queries plus a Korean-flavored query. Found no genuine independent regional benchmark coverage — results were re-syndications of the same automatio.ai-style aggregator content already assessed as non-credible in (4).

(6) Sibling models: Checked the official Google DeepMind model card for the newest sibling, Gemini 3.5 Flash (deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-5-flash/), which explicitly carries a head-to-head comparison column for 'Gemini 3 Flash.' Extracted that column in full: Terminal-bench 2.1 58.0, SWE-Bench Pro (Public) 49.6, MCP Atlas 62.0, Toolathlon 49.4, OSWorld-Verified 65.1, Finance Agent v2 42.6, GDPval-AA 1204, CharXiv 80.3, MMMU-Pro 81.2, Blueprint-Bench 2 0.0, MRCR v2 (128k) 67.2, MRCR v2 (1M) 22.1, Humanity's Last Exam 33.7, ARC-AGI-2 33.6 — this matches the benchmark set already recorded in this corpus file and again contains no MMLU, MATH, HumanEval, or AIME entry, confirming the omission is a genuine Google-wide reporting choice for the whole Gemini 3 family rather than a gap specific to the original launch-day materials already checked. Also fetched the Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite model-evaluation PDF; it was not machine-readable via automated extraction and yielded no usable table.

Conclusion: genuine diligence across all 6 new source types (exceeding the required minimum of 3) surfaced no credible score for 'math' or 'humaneval.' No scores added; both remain a legitimate, well-documented absence.
